Journal.Title: European respiratory review : an official journal of the European Respiratory Society

Article.ArticleTitle: Host- and pathogen-related determinants of pulmonary versus extrapulmonary tuberculosis.

Abstract.AbstractText: Tuberculosis (TB) primarily manifests as pulmonary TB (PTB), but extrapulmonary TB (EPTB) remains a major clinical challenge. Distinct diagnostic and therapeutic difficulties arise from differences in immune responses, pathogen behaviour and host susceptibility. However, the factors driving disease localisation are still incompletely understood. We conducted a comprehensive narrative review of studies examining differences between PTB and EPTB in terms of epidemiology, mycobacterial factors, genetic and epigenetic determinants, host immune responses, transcriptomic profiles, cytokine and chemokine patterns, and immunophenotypes. EPTB is more common among females, children, older adults and immunocompromised individuals with deficient granuloma formation. This review is intended to provide deeper insight for clinicians and researchers and provides an accessible synthesis of current basic science findings together with their relevance for clinical practice. Certain Mycobacterium tuberculosis lineages, notably lineage 1, and specific virulence factors are associated with extrapulmonary dissemination. While genetic polymorphisms influence TB localisation, no studies specifically addressing epigenetic predisposition to EPTB were identified. PTB typically is characterised by T-helper 1-driven immunity, high bacillary loads and robust macrophage activation, whereas EPTB involves compartmentalised immune responses, reduced cytotoxicity and broader cytokine variability. Transcriptomic analyses reveal site-specific gene expression differences and emerging diagnostic blood-based biomarkers show promise but require further validation. Cytokine profiles and immunophenotyping suggest greater immune exhaustion and regulatory T-cell activity in EPTB. We outline practical implications for diagnosis and management and highlight constraints in resource-limited settings and emphasise access and implementation considerations. Integrating these clinical and mechanistic insights can guide more timely recognition and tailored care.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Conflict of interest: B. Tulu, T.T. Brehm, R. van Crevel, T. Dallenga, A.R. DiNardo, K. Dheda, J. Eggeling, W. Enbiale, M.I. Gröschel, J. Hao, V. Kumar, A. van Laarhoven, R. Londt, G. Prosser, and P. Randall have nothing to disclose. N. Reiling reports grants from 2019–2025: DZIF-TTU TB (German Center for Infection Research), 2019–2022: NaPAnti (BMBF, Germany), 2020–2024: MepAnti (EU Horizon 2020, Marie Skłodowska-Curie Action), 2020–2022: GPS-TUBTAR (BMBF, Germany); participation on scientific advisory boards for RTG 2740 Immomicrotope (University Hospital of Erlangen and Friedrich-Alexander-University Erlangen-Nürnberg, Germany) and mycobact2024.se (11th International Conference on the Pathogenesis of Mycobacterial Infections 2024, Stockholm, Sweden); patents granted: EP3481392B1, WO2018007430A1 (“ACC inhibitors for use in treating mycobacterial diseases”); and membership on advisory boards for RTG 2740 Immomicrotope. J. Rybniker reports support for the present study from BMBF (NUM 2.0/NUM 3.0 Funding SNID and NUM-SN); grants from DZIF Flexfund TTU 08.933 (PANTIPA), DFG (RY159/5-1, INST 1856/108-1 LAGG, SFB1403/2, SFB1403/1), BMBF (NUM COVIM 2.0, IDEpiCo), GSK TCOLF (Tres Cantos Open Lab Project TC304), CMMC (B10), EU-Horizon 2020 (ERA4TB), DZIF (TTU-TB 02.814, Flexfund TTU 02.913); consultancy fees from AstraZeneca and Janssen-Cilag GmbH; payment or honoraria for lectures, presentations, manuscript writing or educational events from Westdeutsches Zentrum für Infektiologie, Akademie für Infektionsmedizin Universität Bonn, FOMF, and Janssen-Cilag GmbH; support for attending meetings from Swiss Academic Foundation for Education in ID; patents planned, issued or pending (Verwendung von 1,3,4-Oxadiazolen zur Behandlung von Tuberkulose, DE102023127313.2; Human monoclonal antibodies against P. aeruginosa, UVK29066EP; Benzothiophene and benzyloxybenzylidene and indoline derivatives as pharmaceutically active agents, PCT/EP2015/071110; Benzimidazole sulfide derivatives as pharmaceutically active agents, EP15152650.6); participation on a data safety monitoring board or advisory board with the CESAR consortium (Ruhruniversität Bochum); leadership role with DGI (German Network against Antimicrobial Resistance, DNAMR), FOSA Infectiology in the Network University Medicine (NUM), and as Co-speaker DZIF Translational Thematic Unit Tuberculosis (TTU-TB); and receipt of equipment, materials, drugs, medical writing, gifts or other services from Investitionsmittel UKK (Kamera/ Inkubator Digitalmikroskop, Beckman Cytoflex, 150 °C Ultratiefkühltruhe, 2021–2025). U.E. Schaible has nothing to disclose. E. Schurr reports grants from Canadian Institutes of Health Research, Leprosy Research Initiative and Michael J. Fox Foundation. I. Suarez reports support from DZIF (grant numbers TI 07.001_SUAREZ_00, TI 07.001_SUAREZ_01, and TTU2.913_00) and the Federal Joint Committee (G-BA, German Federal Ministry of Health). S.J. Theobald reports support from a research grant of the CMMC (B10), and stipends from the Imhoff-Stiftung and the Koeln Fortune Program. R.J. Wilkinson reports support for the present study from Francis Crick institute, grants from Wellcome, Cancer Research UK, Research and Innovation, Medical Research Council, European and Developing Countries Clinical Trials Partnership, and National Institutes of Health; and support for attending meetings from Wellcome (payment to institution) and Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation (personal payment). C. Lange reports support for the present study from DZIF (German Center of Infection Research), consultancy fees from INSMED, payment or honoraria for lectures, presentations, manuscript writing or educational events from INSMED, GILEAD, AstraZeneca and GSK, and participation on a data safety monitoring board or advisory board with INSMED and MSF (Medicines sans Frontiers).
